theorem frobenius_bijective_of_finite_field
    {F : Type*} [Field F] [Finite F] {p : Nat} (hp : Nat.Prime p) [CharP F p] :
    Function.Bijective (fun x : F => x ^ p)
node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: first show injectivity. If `x^p = y^p`, then in characteristic `p` we have
`(x - y)^p = x^p - y^p = 0` (all intermediate binomial coefficients are multiples of `p`).
In a field, `z^p = 0` implies `z = 0`, so `x - y = 0` and hence `x = y`. Thus Frobenius is
injective. Since `F` is finite, every injective map `F -> F` is automatically bijective.
